habitat: Moist evergreen forest; rural gardens; parks; plantations; and wet forest understory
story: An icon of the skies, the Sri Lanka Thrush (Zoothera imbricata) is a species from the Turdidae family. It thrives in Moist evergreen forest; rural gardens; parks; plantations; and wet forest understory, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Near Threatened (NT), the Sri Lanka Thrush is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Sri Lanka Thrush helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Sri Lanka Thrush, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
